description: Milestone 13: Similarity Detection & Merge — Implements background keyword matching, vector similarity detection, AI deep comparison, merge request flow, and merge synthesizer agent. Enables automatic detection of similar ideas and collaborative merge workflows.

userStories.title: Background Keyword Matching — Celery Periodic Task
userStories.description: As a system administrator, I want a background service to continuously compare keywords across ideas so that similar ideas can be detected automatically without user intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: Celery periodic task runs on configurable schedule (default: every 5 minutes)
Task queries ideas with keywords updated since last sweep
For each updated idea, compares keyword set against all other ideas within similarity_time_limit (default: 6 months)
Set intersection: if overlap >= min_keyword_overlap (default: 7), triggers similarity.detected event
Checks merge_requests table for previously dismissed pairs (status=declined), skips those pairs
Event payload includes: requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, keyword_overlap_count
Task reads admin_parameters for min_keyword_overlap and similarity_time_limit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § idea_keywords, merge_requests — keyword array, declined pairs tracking
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Background Keyword Matching Service — Celery periodic task design, data access, performance
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § similarity.detected — event payload schema
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-5.2.01, T-5.2.02, T-5.2.03 — threshold trigger, dismissed pair exclusion, time window filter
Files: services/core/background/keyword_matching.py (new), services/core/celeryconfig.py (add task registration)
Gotchas: Celery task scheduling uses crontab() or interval() syntax. Time window filter uses ideas.updated_at. Dismissed pairs are merge_requests with status='declined'. Admin parameters read from admin_parameters table.

data_model: idea_keywords: idea_id uuid UNIQUE, keywords varchar[], last_updated_at timestamptz — one keyword set per idea
merge_requests: requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, status ('pending'|'accepted'|'declined'), merge_type — dual consent tracking
ideas: updated_at timestamptz — filter for similarity_time_limit
admin_parameters: key, value — min_keyword_overlap (default: 7), similarity_time_limit (default: 180 days)
test_cases: T-5.2.01 | Integration | Keyword overlap ≥ threshold triggers similarity.detected | Two ideas with 7+ shared keywords → Event published
T-5.2.02 | Integration | Dismissed pairs not re-matched | Declined merge request exists for pair → No event published
T-5.2.03 | Integration | Time window filter applies | Idea older than similarity_time_limit → Not matched

userStories.title: Vector Similarity Detection — pgvector Cosine Similarity
userStories.description: As a system, I want to detect semantically similar ideas using vector embeddings so that ideas with different keywords but similar meaning can still be matched.
acceptanceCriteria: Celery periodic task queries idea_embeddings table using pgvector cosine similarity
For each updated idea embedding, finds other ideas within similarity_time_limit with cosine similarity >= similarity_vector_threshold (default: 0.75)
Excludes pairs already in merge_requests with status=declined
Publishes similarity.detected event for each match above threshold
Event payload includes: requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, similarity_score
Task reads admin_parameter similarity_vector_threshold
Uses HNSW index on idea_embeddings.embedding for fast similarity search
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § idea_embeddings — embedding vector(1536), source_text_hash, HNSW index
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § pgvector — vector similarity search, HNSW index
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § similarity.detected
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § S6.4 Embedding Generation — idea embedding source text composition
Testing: No specific test IDs assigned (vector similarity is secondary to keyword matching)
Files: services/core/background/vector_similarity.py (new), services/core/celeryconfig.py (add task registration)
Gotchas: pgvector uses <=> operator for cosine similarity. HNSW index already created in migrations. similarity_vector_threshold stored in admin_parameters. Embedding updates happen after keyword generation (handled by AI service).

data_model: idea_embeddings: idea_id uuid UNIQUE, embedding vector(1536), source_text_hash varchar(64), updated_at — semantic embeddings per idea
admin_parameters: similarity_vector_threshold (default: 0.75) — cosine similarity threshold for idea similarity detection

userStories.title: Deep Comparison Agent — AI Similarity Confirmation
userStories.description: As a system, I want an AI agent to perform full-context similarity comparison so that false positives from keyword overlap can be filtered out before notifying users.
acceptanceCriteria: Deep Comparison agent consumes similarity.detected events from message broker
Agent receives idea summaries for both ideas (title + keywords + chat summary + board summary)
System prompt instructs agent to confirm genuine similarity (not generic keyword overlap)
Agent returns structured JSON: {is_similar: bool, confidence: float, explanation: string, overlap_areas: list[str]}
Pydantic validation enforces output schema; retry once on malformed output with format instruction
If is_similar=true and confidence >= 0.7, publishes ai.similarity.confirmed event
If is_similar=false, no further action (silent dismissal)
Uses Default model tier (agent-architecture.md S3.7)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § S3.7 Deep Comparison — purpose, model tier, I/O
AI: docs/03-ai/system-prompts.md § Deep Comparison — full system prompt, output format instructions
AI: docs/03-ai/guardrails.md § Output Validation § 11.6 Deep Comparison — Pydantic schema, retry on malformed output
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § ai.similarity.confirmed — event payload schema
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-5.3.01, T-5.3.02 — structured output validation, Pydantic enforcement
Files: services/ai/agents/deep_comparison/agent.py (new), services/ai/agents/deep_comparison/prompt.py (new), services/ai/consumers/similarity_consumer.py (new)
Gotchas: Agent is non-interactive (no tools). Uses Semantic Kernel basic chat completion. Output Pydantic model: DeepComparisonOutput with is_similar, confidence (0-1), explanation, overlap_areas. Confidence threshold 0.7 is hardcoded (not admin-configurable).

ai_specs: Deep Comparison — model: Default tier (GPT-4o), tools: none, input: two idea summaries, output: JSON {is_similar, confidence, explanation, overlap_areas}
test_cases: T-5.3.01 | AI Agent | Deep Comparison returns structured output | Two similar ideas → {is_similar: bool, confidence: float, explanation, overlap_areas}
T-5.3.02 | AI Agent | Pydantic validation enforces output schema | Malformed output → Retry with format instruction

userStories.title: Similarity Notification Flow — Notify Both Owners
userStories.description: As an idea owner, I want to be notified when my idea is similar to another so that I can decide whether to merge or dismiss the suggestion.
acceptanceCriteria: Notification service consumes ai.similarity.confirmed events
Creates two in-app notifications: one for each idea owner
Notification title: 'Similar idea detected'
Notification body: 'Your idea "[title]" is similar to "[other title]"'
reference_type: 'idea', reference_id: requesting_idea_id (for target owner), target_idea_id (for requesting owner)
Sends email notification to both owners (subject to email_notification_preferences.similarity group)
Email includes: both idea titles, keywords overlap summary, link to requesting idea, link to target idea
Both owners receive read-only access to the other's idea (using share_link_token mechanism from M11)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § ai.similarity.confirmed — event payload
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § notifications, ideas.share_link_token — notification storage, read-only link mechanism
Testing: No specific test IDs (notification flow validated by M12 tests)
Files: services/notification/consumers/similarity_events.py (new or extend existing), services/notification/email/templates/similarity_detected.html (new)
Gotchas: Read-only access uses share_link_token from ideas table (generated on-demand if not exists). Email preference group: 'similarity'. Notification service uses gateway_client.create_notification() gRPC call. Both owners see the other's name (similarity detection is not anonymous).
Deviations: Milestone scope says 'notify both users (in-app + email)' but doesn't specify read-only link generation — this is inferred from F-5.5 step 2 'both users receive read-only access'.

data_model: notifications: user_id, event_type, title, body, reference_id, reference_type, is_read — persistent in-app notifications
ideas: share_link_token varchar(64) UNIQUE — token for read-only sharing (F-8.3), generated on-demand

userStories.title: Similar Ideas Display — GET /api/ideas/:id/similar
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to see similar ideas in the review section so that I can identify potential duplicates or related ideas during the review process.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/ideas/:id/similar endpoint returns list of similar ideas
Response includes: declined merges (merge_requests where status=declined and one idea is :id), near-threshold matches (similarity score between 0.65 and 0.75)
Each entry includes: idea_id, title, keywords, similarity_type ('declined_merge' | 'near_threshold'), similarity_score (for vector matches)
Endpoint requires Bearer token authentication
Only accessible by: idea owner, co-owner, collaborators, assigned reviewers
Returns 403 if user is not authorized to view the idea
Returns 404 if idea does not exist
Response is paginated (default: 10 per page, max: 50)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas/:id/similar — query params, response schema, auth
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § merge_requests, idea_embeddings — declined pairs, vector similarity scores
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.3 Empty States — 'No similar ideas found' when list is empty
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-4.12.01 — API integration test
Files: services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py (add get_similar_ideas view), services/gateway/apps/ideas/urls.py (add similar/ route), services/gateway/apps/ideas/serializers.py (add SimilarIdeaSerializer), services/gateway/apps/ideas/tests/test_similar_ideas.py (new)
Gotchas: 'Near-threshold' matches are ideas with cosine similarity between 0.65 and 0.75 (below confirmation threshold but close). Declined merges are queried from merge_requests table. Requires permission check (same as idea detail endpoint). DRF pagination uses PageNumberPagination.

data_model: merge_requests: requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, status ('declined'), merge_type — declined merge tracking
idea_embeddings: idea_id, embedding vector(1536) — cosine similarity query for near-threshold matches
api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas/:id/similar — Query params: page, page_size. Response: {results: [{id, title, keywords, similarity_type, similarity_score}], count, next, previous}
test_cases: T-4.12.01 | Integration | Similar ideas shown to reviewer | GET /api/ideas/:id/review/similar → 200 + similar ideas list (declined merges + near-threshold)

userStories.title: State-Aware Match Behavior — Open/Rejected Mergeable
userStories.description: As a system, I want similarity detection to respect idea lifecycle states so that only appropriate merge/append actions are offered based on the current state of both ideas.
acceptanceCriteria: Open or Rejected ideas: full merge flow applies (both owners consent, new merged idea created)
In Review ideas: append flow only (open idea owner requests append, in-review owner + one reviewer consent)
Accepted ideas: informational notification only ('similar idea was already accepted, is this a change request or new app?')
Dropped ideas: informational notification only ('similar idea was permanently closed, what's different about yours?')
ai.similarity.confirmed event payload includes both idea states
Notification service logic checks states and creates appropriate notification body
For in_review target, notification includes 'Append request' action (not merge)
For accepted/dropped target, notification is informational with no action buttons
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § ideas.state — state enum values, state transition rules
Design: Feature spec F-5.4 — state-aware match behavior rules (open/rejected→merge, in_review→append, accepted/dropped→informational)
Testing: No specific test IDs (state-aware logic validated by notification service tests)
Files: services/notification/consumers/similarity_events.py (extend from US-004 with state-aware logic)
Gotchas: Append flow (open → in_review) is mentioned in milestone notes as coming in M14. This story only handles the notification differentiation — actual append request API comes in M14. For now, in_review target ideas get informational notification similar to accepted/dropped.
Deviations: Milestone scope says 'state-aware match behavior' but the actual append flow API is deferred to M14. This story implements the notification differentiation only.

userStories.title: Merge Request API — Create Request & Dual Consent Flow
userStories.description: As an idea owner, I want to create a merge request and track consent from both owners so that the merge only proceeds when both parties agree.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/ideas/:id/merge-request creates a merge request
Request body: {target_idea_id: uuid}
Response 201: {id, requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, merge_type: 'merge', status: 'pending', requesting_owner_consent: 'accepted', target_owner_consent: 'pending'}
Requesting owner implicitly consents (requesting_owner_consent='accepted')
Target owner consent defaults to 'pending'
Publishes merge_request.created event for target owner notification
POST /api/merge-requests/:id/consent accepts or declines
Request body: {consent: 'accept' | 'decline'}
On accept: sets target_owner_consent='accepted', checks if both consents are 'accepted', if yes publishes merge_request.accepted event
On decline: sets status='declined', target_owner_consent='declined', publishes merge_request.declined event
Unique constraint: one active merge request per (requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id) pair where status='pending'
Returns 400 if merge request already exists for this pair
Returns 403 if user is not the target idea owner (for consent endpoint)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § POST /api/ideas/:id/merge-request, POST /api/merge-requests/:id/consent — request/response schemas
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § merge_requests — dual consent tracking, unique constraint on active requests
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § merge_request.created, merge_request.accepted, merge_request.declined
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-5.5.01, T-5.5.02, T-5.5.04 — merge request creation, target locking, decline dismissal
Files: services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py (add create_merge_request view), services/gateway/apps/merge/views.py (new, add consent view), services/gateway/apps/merge/urls.py (new), services/gateway/apps/merge/serializers.py (new), services/gateway/apps/merge/tests/test_merge_request.py (new)
Gotchas: Requesting owner implicitly consents (requesting_owner_consent='accepted' on creation). Unique constraint uq_active_merge_request enforced at DB level. Event publishing uses gateway events/publisher.py pattern from M12.

data_model: merge_requests: id uuid PK, requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, merge_type ('merge'|'append'), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'declined'), requesting_owner_consent ('accepted'|'pending'), target_owner_consent ('accepted'|'pending'|'declined'), resulting_idea_id, created_at, resolved_at
Constraint: uq_active_merge_request UNIQUE (requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id) WHERE status='pending'
api_endpoints: POST /api/ideas/:id/merge-request — Body: {target_idea_id} → 201 {id, requesting_idea_id, target_idea_id, merge_type, status, consents}
test_cases: T-5.5.01 | Integration | Merge request creates pending record | POST /api/ideas/:id/merge-request → 201, merge_request with status=pending
T-5.5.02 | Integration | Target idea locked with merge request | Active merge request → merge_request_pending populated in idea response
T-5.5.04 | Integration | Decline permanently dismisses pair | POST /api/merge-requests/:id/decline → Status=declined, pair never suggested again

userStories.title: Merge Request Banner — Workspace UI with Accept/Decline
userStories.description: As a target idea owner, I want to see a merge request banner in my idea workspace so that I can accept or decline the merge request without leaving the workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: MergeRequestBanner component renders when merge_request_pending is present in idea API response
Banner displays: 'Merge request from [requesting owner name]: merge your idea with "[requesting idea title]"'
Two buttons: 'Accept' (primary) and 'Decline' (ghost)
Banner is full-width below workspace header, uses border-b bg-warning/5 per component-specs.md § 11.5
Accept button calls POST /api/merge-requests/:id/consent with {consent: 'accept'}, shows loading state
Decline button calls POST /api/merge-requests/:id/consent with {consent: 'decline'}, shows confirmation dialog first
On accept: banner disappears, toast 'Merge request accepted. Creating merged idea...', idea unlocks
On decline: banner disappears, toast 'Merge request declined', idea unlocks
Idea is locked (chat + board read-only) while merge request is pending
LockOverlay component wraps entire workspace with message: 'This idea has a pending merge request. Accept or decline to continue editing.'
WebSocket event ws:merge_request updates merge_request_pending field and triggers banner re-render
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.5 Banners — workspace-integrated banner variant (border-b bg-warning/5), framer-motion slide-down
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 5.8 Chat Input Area — LockOverlay pattern for idea locking
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/ideas/:id — merge_request_pending field in response
Testing: No specific test IDs (UI component validated by frontend unit tests)
Files: frontend/src/components/workspace/MergeRequestBanner.tsx (new), frontend/src/pages/IdeaWorkspace.tsx (integrate banner + lock overlay), frontend/src/hooks/use-websocket.ts (add ws:merge_request event handler)
Gotchas: Banner is persistent (not dismissible) until resolved. Lock overlay prevents all editing (chat input disabled, board canvas read-only). Confirmation dialog for decline uses Dialog component (not AlertDialog — none exists per component-specs.md § 17). merge_request_pending is a nested object: {id, requesting_idea_id, requesting_owner_name, requesting_idea_title}.

api_endpoints: GET /api/ideas/:id — Response includes merge_request_pending: {id, requesting_idea_id, requesting_owner_name, requesting_idea_title} | null
components: MergeRequestBanner — full-width banner below header, accept/decline buttons, lock explanation
LockOverlay — from component-specs.md § 5.8, wraps workspace with bg-card/80 + message

userStories.title: Merge Synthesizer Agent — Synthesis Message & Board Instructions
userStories.description: As a system, I want an AI agent to synthesize two merged ideas into a combined context so that the new merged idea starts with a coherent summary and organized board.
acceptanceCriteria: Merge Synthesizer agent consumes merge_request.accepted events
Agent receives full context for both ideas: title, keywords, chat summary, board state (nodes + connections)
System prompt instructs agent to create: (1) synthesis message summarizing both ideas' combined context, (2) board instructions for merged board layout
Agent returns structured JSON: {synthesis_message: string, board_instructions: array}
synthesis_message is a chat message that becomes the first message in the new merged idea
board_instructions is an array of board modification instructions (same format as Facilitator's request_board_changes tool)
Pydantic validation enforces output schema; retry once on malformed output
Agent uses Default model tier (agent-architecture.md S3.9)
On success, publishes merge_synthesizer.complete event with synthesis output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § S3.9 Merge Synthesizer — purpose, model tier, I/O, tool-free agent
AI: docs/03-ai/system-prompts.md § Merge Synthesizer — full system prompt, synthesis instructions, board deduplication rules
AI: docs/03-ai/tools-and-functions.md § request_board_changes — board instruction format (used as output template)
AI: docs/03-ai/guardrails.md § Output Validation § 11.6 Merge Synthesizer — Pydantic schema
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § merge_synthesizer.complete
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-5.5.05 — synthesis + board instructions output
Files: services/ai/agents/merge_synthesizer/agent.py (new), services/ai/agents/merge_synthesizer/prompt.py (new), services/ai/consumers/merge_consumer.py (new)
Gotchas: Agent is non-interactive (no tools). Output Pydantic model: MergeSynthesizerOutput with synthesis_message (string) and board_instructions (list[dict]). board_instructions use same schema as Facilitator's request_board_changes tool (intent, description, suggested_title, etc.).

ai_specs: Merge Synthesizer — model: Default tier (GPT-4o), tools: none, input: two idea contexts, output: JSON {synthesis_message, board_instructions}
test_cases: T-5.5.05 | AI Agent | Merge Synthesizer produces synthesis + board instructions | Two idea contexts → {synthesis_message: string, board_instructions: array}

userStories.title: Merged Idea Creation — New Idea with Co-Owners & Transferred Collaborators
userStories.description: As a system, I want to create a new merged idea when both owners accept a merge request so that the combined idea workspace preserves all context and collaborators from both original ideas.
acceptanceCriteria: Core service consumes merge_synthesizer.complete events
Creates new idea: title = 'Merged: [idea1 title] + [idea2 title]', state = 'open', visibility = 'collaborating'
Sets owner_id = requesting idea owner, co_owner_id = target idea owner (dual ownership)
Sets merged_from_idea_1_id = requesting_idea_id, merged_from_idea_2_id = target_idea_id
First chat message: synthesis_message from Merge Synthesizer, sender_type='ai', ai_agent='merge_synthesizer'
Board Agent processes board_instructions from Merge Synthesizer to create merged board
Transfers all collaborators from both ideas to new idea (INSERT into idea_collaborators, deduplicated by user_id)
Updates both original ideas: closed_by_merge_id = new_merged_idea_id, state remains unchanged (original state preserved)
Updates merge_request: status='accepted', resulting_idea_id = new_merged_idea_id, resolved_at = now()
Publishes merge.complete event for both owners + all collaborators notification
Both original ideas become read-only: chat locked, board locked, message: 'This idea was merged. View merged idea: [link]'
Entire operation is atomic (transaction rollback on any failure)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § ideas — merged_from_idea_1_id, merged_from_idea_2_id, closed_by_merge_id, co_owner_id (dual ownership for merged ideas)
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § idea_collaborators — transfer all collaborators, deduplicate by user_id
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § chat_messages — synthesis message as first AI message
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § S4.4 Board Agent — processes board instructions from Merge Synthesizer
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Message Broker Event Contracts § merge.complete — notify all users
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-5.5.03, T-5.5.06 — synthesis trigger, full E2E merge flow
Files: services/core/consumers/merge_consumer.py (new), services/core/services/merge_service.py (new)
Gotchas: Atomic transaction required (Django transaction.atomic() or gRPC transaction). Board Agent invoked in-process (not via message broker). Old ideas remain accessible via read-only link (share_link_token). Collaborator transfer uses UNION of both idea_collaborators sets, excluding the two owners (they become co-owners). Title 'Merged:' prefix indicates merged idea.
Deviations: Milestone scope says 'AI synthesizes a summary of merged context as the first chat message' — implemented as synthesis_message from Merge Synthesizer agent.

data_model: ideas: owner_id, co_owner_id (merged ideas only), merged_from_idea_1_id, merged_from_idea_2_id, closed_by_merge_id — dual ownership + merge tracking
idea_collaborators: idea_id, user_id, joined_at — collaborator join table, deduplicated by user_id
chat_messages: idea_id, sender_type ('ai'), ai_agent ('merge_synthesizer'), content — synthesis message as first message
merge_requests: status ('accepted'), resulting_idea_id, resolved_at — merge completion tracking
ai_specs: Board Agent — processes board_instructions array from Merge Synthesizer (create_node, move_node, etc.)
test_cases: T-5.5.03 | Integration | Accept merge triggers synthesis | POST /api/merge-requests/:id/accept → Event published, resulting_idea created
T-5.5.06 | E2E | Full merge flow end-to-end | Two similar ideas → detect → request → accept → merged idea | Merged idea created with combined content
